name: Charred New Potato and Fennel Salad
recipeIngredient: 2 medium Fennel bulbs; trimmed, quartered lengthwise
recipeInstructions:
Preparation time: 25 minutes. Cooking time: 25 minutes.
1. Set broiler rack about 6 inches from heat. Turn on the broiler.
2. Toss potatoes and fennel in 1/4 cup olive oil; reserve any extra oil.
Spread in a single layer on the broiler pan. Lightly season with salt and pepper.
Broil until blistered and slightly blackened, turning once, about 5 minutes per
side.
3. Bake in a preheated 400-degree oven until just tender, about 15-20 minutes.
Do not overcook potatoes.
4. When cool enough to handle, cut potatoes and fennel into 1/2-inch dice.
Toss with remaining olive oil, 6 tablespoons vinegar, and the chopped green onions
and olives. Season to taste. Add additional vinegar if needed.
5. Can be made several hours ahead and kept at room temperature or refrigerated,
covered airtight. Let come to room temperature before serving.
6. Adjust seasoning.
This salad would pair well with grilled meat or poultry. For best flavor, prepare
several hours in advance so the flavors have time to meld together.
